AccountHoldings.description: One investment account with its funds rolled up.

annualisedPct.description: Account-level CAGR %, or null when too short / unknown.

currency.description: The shared currency of all funds in this account, or null if mixed.

heldYears.description: Years the account has been tracked/held, or null.

inceptionSource.description: Where `inception` came from: a manual seed vs the earliest observed sync.

returnPct.description: Cumulative return %: totalReturn/totalCost for a single-currency account, else a value-weighted blend of the per-fund %s (the native dollar totals can't be summed across currencies — suppress those, not this).

AgentReportResponse.description: POST /api/agent-report — persist + deliver monthly review report

AllocationFork.description: The final mortgage-vs-investments fork as a head-to-head: route the spare to whichever reaches FI sooner. Both sides carry their FI outcome so a surface can show "if invest → X | if mortgage → Y" rather than only the winner.

monthsSooner.description: invest.fiMonths − payMortgage.fiMonths (months). >0 ⇒ paying down reaches FI sooner; <0 ⇒ investing does; null when an arm doesn't reach FI in horizon.

mortgageVsInvest.description: Head-to-head behind the final rung (the same simulation that picked `choice`).

ApiError.description: Canonical error body shared by all routes

AssetsUpsertResponse.description: POST /api/assets — create or update a manual asset

BudgetHistoryResponse.description: GET /api/budgets/history — budget snapshot history. With `category` param: CategoryHistory. Without: { cycles: CycleHistory[] }.

pendingSpent.description: Provisional pending (unsettled) net-outflow attributed to this category by the rule engine — money spent at the bank but not yet settled. Additive to `effectiveSpend` for a true "committed so far" view; 0 when none. Not part of the audited `spent`/`pct`/`status`, which stay settled-only.

BudgetsResponse.description: GET /api/budgets (no `category` param). Returns a subset of BudgetComputeResult: period, flex, inbox, position, and the (optionally group-filtered) budget rows. Does NOT include shadowCommitted, unallocatedPending, or reserveBuffer.

category.description: Resolved category name, present when the caller used the name-based path.

similarMerchant.description: Merchant to pass to applySimilar when the learned rule is merchant-based; null for description rules or bulk/uncategorise.

spend.description: Net outflow that day (Σ -amount over monthly_cap txns); negative on a refund day.

cyclePerDay.description: spentSoFar ÷ dayOfPeriod — the whole-cycle average daily pace.

dayOfPeriod.description: Day number within the cycle as of `now` (1-based).

plannedPerDay.description: Σ active monthly_cap targets ÷ periodLength — the planned daily drag (matches the forecast).

priorPerDay.description: Average over the `trailingDays` days before the trailing window; null when the cycle is too young to compare.

spentSoFar.description: Σ days.spend — variable spend so far this cycle.

trailingDays.description: Window size actually used (clamped to elapsed days).

trailingPerDay.description: Average daily burn over the last `trailingDays` days — the headline pace.

vsPlan.description: trailingPerDay − plannedPerDay; positive = burning hotter than plan.

configured.description: Whether an account is designated as the emergency fund.

actualReturnAnnualisedPct.description: The same portfolio's annualised (CAGR) return as a fraction, or null when no investment account has a usable inception date. Companion to actualReturnPct — the per-year rate the cumulative figure works out to.

actualReturnPct.description: Cumulative ("since purchase") return of the FI investment portfolio, as a fraction (0.15 = +15%). Value-weighted blend across investment accounts (KiwiSaver excluded — locked until 65). Backward-looking reality check, NOT fed into the projection.

ForecastAssumptions.description: What the forecast is based on — surfaced so the UI can show a "what this assumes" panel (and the user can spot a wrong inference). All of it is auto-derived.

bills.description: Each committed bill's seeded post-day + amount, and where the seed came from.

dailyBurn.description: Combined monthly_cap allowance ÷ cycle length — the daily discretionary drag.

income.description: One entry per detected pay stream (or a single monthly-budget fallback).

billsDue.description: The next major bill cluster the verdict is judged against (null => payday fallback).

events.description: The dated drivers behind the walk (pay/bills/daily burn), date-sorted.

HoldingView.description: A single fund prepared for display.

annualisedPct.description: Compound annual growth rate %, or null when too short / unknown.

inception.description: Effective inception used for annualisation (`yyyy-mm-dd`), or null.

InvestmentInceptionResponse.description: PATCH /api/investments/inception — set account's manual investing-since date

InvestmentsResponse.description: GET /api/investments — investment holdings grouped by account + portfolio roll-up

PortfolioSummary.description: Whole-portfolio roll-up across every investment/KiwiSaver account.

annualisedCoverageNZD.description: NZD value of the accounts the annualised blend covers (≤ valueNZD); less than valueNZD when some accounts still need an investing-since date.

annualisedPct.description: NZD-value-weighted blend of per-account annualised (CAGR) %, or null when no account has a usable inception date.

returnPct.description: NZD-value-weighted blend of per-account cumulative return %, or null.

valueNZD.description: Total NZD value across all accounts that hold funds.

pending.description: Net pending (unsettled) outflow folded into `projected`; 0 if none.

planned.description: Structural plan-vs-plan surplus/deficit: Σ income budgets − Σ expense budgets. Unlike `actual`/`projected` (which blend in this cycle's real spend & receipts), this answers the design-time question "do the budgets I've SET commit me to spending more than I plan to earn?" — independent of how any one cycle is tracking. Negative ⇒ over-committed budgets.

mortgageFreeMonths.description: When this arm's mortgage clears (months from now / "YYYY-MM").

freedPayment.description: Scheduled P&I that frees up at payoff and redirects to investing.

investArm.description: Keep investing the extra; mortgage runs to its scheduled payoff.

monthsSooner.description: investArm.fiMonths − payMortgageArm.fiMonths. >0 ⇒ paying down reaches FI sooner. null when either arm doesn't reach FI within the horizon.

mortgage.description: The mortgage-only what-if (free-date shift + lifetime interest saved) from the extra repayment — so the panel needn't recompute it.

payMortgageArm.description: Throw the extra (and any lump) at the mortgage; clears sooner.

verdict.description: Which arm wins on the FI date. "tie" when equal (or neither reaches).

SetBudgetTargetResponse.description: PATCH /api/budgets/target (success branch). The route returns `{ category, ...result }` where result is `{ ok: true; previousTarget: number; newTarget: number }`.

pending.description: True for provisional pending (unsettled) rows attributed by the rule engine — rendered with a "pending" hint and never persisted.
